item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There seems to be a really poor grasp of distance here. As far as I can tell, Bitterroot to Belgrade is a couple of hours by car; even if the D-class found a car they'd have to drive at well over 100 mph to get there in 82 minutes. Also, Belgrade is east of the forest, not north.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Several things I'm not sure about in this article.

1. A D-class who's crime was vandalism. It is my understanding that D-class are typically people who have committed heinous crimes such as rape, murder, etc. After all the D stands for "disposable" and if someone is supposed to get out of jail any time soon, they aren't really disposable.

2. "Approximately 7 nails" this reads like the person writing the report can't count to 7, or that the nails change states between broken and unbroken. If that's the case, fine, but the adjective "approximately" is typically used in articles to qualify something that is hard to calculate precisely, like a percentage of surface area, something so massive in number that it is hard to count (much more numborous than 7), or something that changes quantity anomalously but tends to float around one state in particular.

3. Bitterroot national Forest is a fairly large place, at 6,423 km². So when you say that the foundation has re-directed trails from the scp to "the entrance" that sounds a bit wrong. Surely a place so large does not just have 1 entrance.

4. The dialogue between D-class and researcher is very sparse. D-class goes all the way to the state border (without being told to) and doesn't say anything to the researcher the whole time. Not only this but we aren't even told that some dialogue may be removed for brevity, so the audio version of this logue would be majority silence as no-one talks for some reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it (the current version), it's nice and simple, with a creepy undertone, and the formatting was done very well, though it does have a few issues with it. One, I don't really see what the voices/attacks have to do with the pocket dimension, they just don't seem related, or if they are it's not communicated very well. I also think you could have added a bit more to the pocket dimension part, it just seems to be lacking some detail. Overall it was pretty good, nice work (+1).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Few typo's I've noticed:

"individuals will experience multiple voices, commonly under within the age range of 10-65 years old"
Do you mean under 10 or between 10 and 65?

"SCP-4370 will function as a non-anomalous playground slide until the individual reaches ground level, where they will enter SCP-4370-1 if the sliding process has not been interrupted by an outside force. Interruption of the sliding process may negate SCP-4370’s anomalous properties."
This just doesn't seem to read correctly. The last sentence makes me think that any interruption to someone sliding could stop 4370 from ever working again, while the previous sentence seems needlessly long, but that's just me.

+1 because I like the idea and execution.

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Currently, you have not sourced the image used in this article, and as such may be a violation of the SCP wiki's license (CC-BY-SA 3.0). You have 24 hours to source the image used. If you have not sourced the image within the allotted time frame, or the source is noncompliant with the SCP wiki's license, then we'll have to take the image down.

EDIT: The image has been sourced, and is cc-compliant. As such, it can remain on this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I want to like this one, I really do. However, it just never really coalesced into anything for me. The anomalous properties of the slide itself seem to be unrelated to the extra dimension, unless I missed something. Nothing really comes out of the exploration log, and the dado-like "FUN SLIDE" sign just seems to contradict the tone of the rest of the SCP. The ending list of recovered materials didn't seem to make anything more clear, and I'm unsure what emotion the SCP overall wants me to feel.

Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey, thanks for reading!

This is my third SCP, and hoo boy, this one took a while. It was originally going to be a stone eye that teleported people to another dimension, but I decided against that since I felt it wasn't interesting enough.

Big shoutout to the Drafts and Critiques forum for their contributions, specifically elycat!

Feel free to leave any feedback or criticism you may have, I'll try my best to reply as soon as I can!

Until next time!

Edit (3/19/19): I'm currently rewriting this, as it only has one upvote. You can critique and/or view it on my fourth sandbox page.

Credits:

Image Source: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Water_Slide_(59872814).jpeg
